page.title: Advanced Participle And Absolute Clauses
page.subtitle_html: Advanced Participle And Absolute Clauses - C2 reduced clauses for sequence, reason, concession, and circumstance.
page.subtitle_text: C2 reduced clauses for sequence, reason, concession, and circumstance.

blocks.body: {"level":"C2","intro":"Advanced participle and absolute clauses: compact anchors for reduced clauses that condense time, reason, concession, and circumstances.","rules":[{"label":"Perfect participle","text":"Use Having + V3 for an earlier completed action.","example":"Having finished the report, she sent it to the manager."},{"label":"Past participle clause","text":"Use V3 clauses where the noun is affected by the action.","example":"Written in clear language, the policy was easy to follow."},{"label":"Absolute clause","text":"Use noun + participle to add an independent circumstance.","example":"All conditions having been met, the contract became valid."}]}

blocks.heading: C2 focus
blocks.body: This compact page anchors the Polyglot English C2 lesson on advanced participle and absolute clauses.
